summary: Soundboard capture of the second half delivers stellar performances (St. Stephen, The Eleven, Dark Star, The Other One) yet is incomplete compared to the full Cotsman version.
review: The koucky recording showcases a high‑energy mid‑summer ’68 show with standout moments like an inspired Eleven jam, a beautifully voiced Death Don't Have No Mercy, and a dark, tightly crafted Dark Star. Audio quality is decent for a soundboard source but the set is missing the first half and some of the night’s best material, so listeners are urged to seek the more complete Cotsman version.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviewers praise the musical performance strongly but note that the recording is incomplete and some vocal levels are low, pulling the overall rating down.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Decent clarity for a soundboard capture; Jerry's vocals are low on St. Stephen, and the tape only contains the second half of the show, omitting early highlights.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong mid‑set flow with seamless transitions (e.g., The Eleven into Death, and The Other One into New Potato), though the recording stops before the first half concludes.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocal volume low on St. Stephen but powerful later; guitar duels with Phil in The Eleven.
band_member_comments.Phil: Engages in tight duel during The Eleven, zooms on bass throughout, and delivers a rare extended solo after New Potato.
band_member_comments.Keys: Organ adds perfect embellishments on Death Don't Have No Mercy.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (Kreutzmann) goes 'nuts' on drums, driving the high‑energy jams.
